production_fidelity_environment.engine: static-inspection + structural code trace (no live Firebase project, no live browser instance available in this sandboxed verifier)
production_fidelity_environment.rationale: This run executed inside a non-interactive Windows file-tree harness with no spawnable browser (jsdom/Playwright/Puppeteer) and no live Firebase project; Tier 1 PNV.1 cannot be executed end-to-end live in this environment. Per the v1.9 honest-reporting requirement, the verifier declares each external component below as real or modeled, with the static-inspection harness exercised against the integrated artifact at output/integration/. The Coordinator-reported edge-case-testing report (32/32 PASS, runtime ESM module execution against the integration directory) is cited as the closest available production-fidelity behavioral evidence under the constraint.

components.name: browser_runtime
components.rationale: No jsdom/Playwright/Puppeteer instance was spun up by this verifier role; the edge-case-testing builder DID run a Node.js ESM harness that imported the integration's modules (state.js could not import firebase-firestore CDN inside Node, so it shimmed those imports per its test-suite.mjs; pure-logic modules like flow.js, stats.js, scenarios.js were exercised real).
components.source: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/script and https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/scripting.html#the-script-element (HTML/JS module loading semantics relied on for static trace of app.js boot path)

components.name: firebase_sdk
components.rationale: Vendored from https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.12.5/* via ES module imports in src/state.js. Module specifiers, exported function names (initializeApp, getAuth, signInAnonymously, getFirestore, doc, setDoc, getDoc, updateDoc, onSnapshot, serverTimestamp, runTransaction), and call-site signatures were verified by static inspection against the published Firebase JS SDK v10 documentation.

components.name: firestore_backend
components.rationale: No live Firebase project is bound by this verifier; the integrated artifact's adapter targets Firestore via the SDK calls listed above. Security Rules in firestore.rules were inspected for syntactic conformance and predicate correctness against Firestore Rules v2 grammar. A live emulator run was not performed by this role; the edge-case-testing report verified rule-text invariants (participantUids array predicate, size()==1 create predicate, size()==2 max-update predicate) via text-matching.

components.name: firebase_anonymous_auth
components.rationale: Anonymous Auth is invoked via firebase-auth.js#signInAnonymously and onAuthStateChanged. Call-site, idempotency guard, and the auth-resolves-before-UI-mount ordering were verified by static inspection of src/state.js and src/app.js boot sequence. No live UID was issued.

components.name: web_push_service
components.rationale: Push pipeline (PushManager.subscribe with userVisibleOnly + applicationServerKey, VAPID-signed JWT in browser via SubtleCrypto ECDSA P-256, POST to subscription.endpoint with Authorization: vapid t=<jwt>, k=<vapid-public-key> and TTL header) was inspected against the W3C Web Push protocol spec and RFC 8292 (VAPID). No actual push delivery was attempted. sw.js push and notificationclick event handlers were inspected for structural conformance to the Push API spec.

components.name: service_worker_registration_path
components.rationale: Static inspection of sw.js (root of integration/) and the registration call in src/push.js (navigator.serviceWorker.register('./sw.js', { scope: './' })) is reliable: the relative URL resolves to the entrypoint's directory at runtime, giving the service worker scope = the GitHub Pages project subpath. This is a deterministic property of the W3C Service Worker spec's URL resolution rules.

components.name: github_pages_static_host
components.rationale: The artifact's deployability to GitHub Pages was verified by static inspection: no build step required (no package.json scripts the user must run; no Node modules to install); index.html plus relative-path asset graph; manifest.json and sw.js at the root level for correct subpath scoping; firestore.rules separated as a one-time-paste file. No live publish was performed.

first_contact_results.description: Static directory committable to gh-pages branch with no build step.
first_contact_results.evidence: Integration directory contains only static assets (index.html, sw.js, manifest.json, firestore.rules, README.md, src/*.js, styles/app.css, data/scenarios.json, icons/*.svg). Zero package.json files exist at the integration root. No build script the user must run. All JS is ES modules loaded directly via <script type=module>. Firebase SDK is loaded from gstatic CDN at runtime, not bundled.

first_contact_results.description: Landing screen with 'Create a new game' affordance loads without console errors.
first_contact_results.evidence: Traced src/app.js boot(): loadScenarios -> initFirebase -> signInAnonymously -> registerServiceWorker -> mountRouter -> goLanding -> mountLandingView. mountLandingView (src/onboarding.js:44) renders a button 'Create a new game' (line 58). With placeholder Firebase config (apiKey starts with 'PASTE_'), the renderConfigError() branch is hit instead (app.js:56-58), surfacing a user-readable 'Setup needed' screen — this is intentional and matches FC.2's 'assuming user has already configured Firebase' rider. No throw paths reach the user on a fresh load.

first_contact_results.description: Create-game produces a shareable identifier; join URL pattern implemented.
first_contact_results.evidence: src/state.js#createGame generates a 6-char base32 share code (line 116-126) and a join URL of the form '<origin><pathname>?join=<code>' (buildJoinUrl, line 128-134). src/onboarding.js#mountWaitingForOpponentView renders the share code and join URL with copy-to-clipboard affordances (lines 243-267). src/app.js initial route resolution reads ?join= from URL.searchParams and routes to mountJoinGameView with prefilled code (lines 164-168).

first_contact_results.description: Scenario screen requires action + confidence before submit.
first_contact_results.evidence: src/ui.js#renderMyTurn validation loop (lines 176-186): rejects submission with 'Pick an action for scenario N' if !s.action, then 'Pick a confidence (1-5) for scenario N' if !s.confidence. Submission only proceeds after both gates pass. Note input is optional (no validation gate, accepts up to 280 chars).

first_contact_results.description: No absolute-root asset paths; subpath-safe asset graph.
first_contact_results.evidence: grep for src="/ and href="/ across output/integration returned zero matches. index.html uses ./manifest.json, ./icons/icon-192.svg, ./styles/app.css, ./src/app.js. scenarios.js loads ../data/scenarios.json via new URL('../data/scenarios.json', import.meta.url) (subpath-safe per import.meta semantics). sw.js icon refs use ./icons/icon-192.svg. Service worker registers with relative './sw.js' (push.js:82).

first_contact_results.description: Notification opt-in: permission request -> subscribe -> store endpoint; sw.js handles push event.
first_contact_results.evidence: src/push.js#enableNotifications gated on platformSupportsPush() and on iOS standalone state. Notification.requestPermission() invoked at line 127, called from a user-click handler bound in src/ui.js#buildNotifControl (line 310). On grant: reg.pushManager.subscribe with VAPID public key (lines 141-144); subscription serialized to PushSubscriptionRecord shape (endpoint + keys.p256dh + keys.auth + subscribed_at) and persisted via savePushSubscription (line 157). sw.js registers 'push' event handler (line 14) that renders self.registration.showNotification with title/body/tag/icon, and a 'notificationclick' handler (line 40) that focuses/opens the game tab.

first_contact_results.description: Mobile: viewport meta + responsive CSS + tappable controls.
first_contact_results.evidence: index.html line 5: <meta name='viewport' content='width=device-width, initial-scale=1, viewport-fit=cover'>. styles/app.css line 341: @media (max-width: 480px) overrides for .input-row, .stats-row, .stat-block, .app-title, .action-btn (full-width on mobile). Action buttons are <button> elements (not custom DOM), inherently tappable.

first_contact_results.description: Wrap-up screen: individual + agreement stats + highest-joint-confidence-disagreement list.
first_contact_results.evidence: src/ui.js#mountWrapUpView (line 339+) renders three sections: accuracySection (per-player accuracy via stats.perPlayerAccuracy), agreementSection (inter-player agreement via stats.interPlayerAgreement), disagreementSection (ranked list via stats.rankedDisagreements ordered by joint_confidence_min descending with tie-break by joint_confidence_sum then scenario_id, per src/stats.js#rankedDisagreements lines 101-105). Edge-case-testing report confirms ranking ordering: 'S4.A7' shows minima=[5,3,2] in actual output order.

prompt_named_verb_result.verb: play (a complete asynchronous head-to-head GTO duel)
prompt_named_verb_result.rationale: PNV.1 requires two browser profiles + live Firebase + live Web Push endpoint to execute end-to-end. This verifier role does not have a spawnable browser or a live Firebase project in its execution context. Per the v1.9 honest-reporting directive, the verification falls back to: (a) static-trace of the full 9-step PNV.1 scenario code path; (b) citing the edge-case-testing report (32/32 PASS) which exercised every reachable structural and pure-logic clause of PNV.1 via a Node ESM harness against the integration directory; (c) declaring firestore_backend and web_push_service as `modeled` with Firebase canonical docs and W3C Web Push spec cited above.

static_trace_clauses.clause: Profile A creates a game, sees a share code on a 'waiting for opponent' screen.
static_trace_clauses.evidence: src/state.js#createGame -> src/app.js#goWaitingOrGame -> src/onboarding.js#mountWaitingForOpponentView. Share code surfaced via shareCode and joinUrl in waiting-for-opponent view.

static_trace_clauses.clause: Profile B opens the share URL and joins.
static_trace_clauses.evidence: URL ?join=<code> parsed in src/app.js initial route (line 164); src/onboarding.js#mountJoinGameView pre-fills the code (line 158); state.js#joinGame uses a Firestore runTransaction to atomically add the second UID to participantUids and switch status to 'in_progress' (lines 199-240).

static_trace_clauses.clause: Round 1, Player A submits a handful with action + confidence + optional note; GTO answer hidden during input.
static_trace_clauses.evidence: src/ui.js#renderMyTurn renders per-scenario card with: description, board, action_history, available_actions buttons, 1-5 confidence buttons, optional note textarea (maxlength 280). GTO answer is rendered ONLY in renderReveal (line 245+), not in renderMyTurn.

static_trace_clauses.clause: Profile A shown 'waiting for Player B' screen.
static_trace_clauses.evidence: src/ui.js#renderWaiting (line 233) and computePhase's waitingForOpponent branch (src/flow.js line 57). The Firestore snapshot received by Profile A is redacted by state.js#redactGameForViewer (lines 413-431) so Player B's pre-submission state is masked client-side as defense-in-depth.

static_trace_clauses.clause: Profile B receives push notification (when opted in and supported).
static_trace_clauses.evidence: src/ui.js submitHandful success handler calls sendTurnNotification(gameId, oppUid, ...) (line 206). src/push.js#sendTurnNotification reads opponent's stored PushSubscriptionRecord, signs a VAPID JWT, POSTs to the subscription endpoint. sw.js push event handler renders the OS notification. Modeled — not actually delivered in this verifier's environment, but the wire path conforms to the W3C Web Push spec.

static_trace_clauses.clause: Profile B sees the same Round 1 scenarios as Profile A.
static_trace_clauses.evidence: Scenarios are precomputed in state.js#precomputeRounds at game-creation time (line 392+) using a deterministic per-round seed (gameId#round-N). Both UIDs read from the same round.scenarioIds array. Confirmed by edge-case-testing report's DCA.5 evidence block (both phases show identical scenarioIds list).

static_trace_clauses.clause: Round 1 reveal: per-scenario, each player's action + confidence + note + GTO-verified action.
static_trace_clauses.evidence: src/ui.js#renderReveal (line 245+) iterates round.scenarioIds and renders subA/subB action with aRight/bRight styling, confidence number, player_note (if present), GTO action + explanation.

static_trace_clauses.clause: Round 2 begins with Player B leading (alternation per A17).
static_trace_clauses.evidence: src/state.js#joinGame (lines 223-226): newRounds = (data.rounds || []).map((r, i) => ({ ...r, leaderUid: i % 2 === 0 ? uids[0] : uid })). Round 0 leader = creator (uid_a); Round 1 leader = joiner (uid_b). flow.js#computePhase determines myTurn based on first round where viewer's submission count < expected, naturally enforcing the alternation pattern.

static_trace_clauses.clause: End-of-game wrap-up: individual performance + inter-player agreement + ranked joint-confidence-min disagreements.
static_trace_clauses.evidence: Already traced under FC.8. Edge-case-testing report's DCA.10, DCA.11, S4.A7, IP8.A2 all PASS with concrete minima=[5,3,2] ordering observed.

static_trace_clauses.clause: Zero outbound network requests fire to any origin other than chosen BaaS endpoints and Web Push subscription endpoints.
static_trace_clauses.evidence: Static inspection of all *.js in src/: only network egress goes to (a) https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/* (SDK load), (b) Firebase project (Firestore + Auth via SDK), (c) push subscription endpoint via fetch in push.js#sendTurnNotification line 241, (d) ./data/scenarios.json (same-origin). No third-party analytics, telemetry, or CDN requests.

prompt_named_verb_result.edge_case_report_reference: 32/32 PASS at output/builders/edge-case-testing/edge-tests/report.json. The PNV.1 entry specifically records 'Production-fidelity dynamic execution deferred to first deploy' with structural_clause_count=10. This matches the v1.9 honest-reporting contract: the user-facing first-contact is exercised live by the user themselves on their own deployed instance; the architecture's job is to verify deliverable correctness, not to fully simulate user setup.

assumption_checks.evidence: No companion server, no Dockerfile, no package.json with build scripts. Only static files + firestore.rules paste-file.
Firestore Rules cap participantUids at size 2 on update; joinGame returns error:'game_full' if uids.length >= 2 (state.js:212). Confirmed by edge-case report DCA.4.
Game state lives in Firestore; readGame uses onSnapshot for live updates; submitHandful is idempotent; no synchronous-both-online requirement anywhere.
Scenarios precomputed at game creation via deterministic seed; both UIDs read same round.scenarioIds array (state.js#precomputeRounds).
Handful size configurable 1-10 in create-game form (onboarding.js:89, clamped in createGame at state.js:150).
Round count configurable 1-10 at game creation; flow.js#computePhase returns gameComplete=true when all rounds are fully submitted; ui.js routes to wrap-up on gameComplete.
Per-decision confidence 1-5 required by ui.js validation gate (line 182) and by state.js#submitHandful schema check (line 299-302).
stats.js#rankedDisagreements filters to action mismatches and orders by joint_confidence_min (min of c_a, c_b) descending. Edge report S4.A7 confirms ordering.
ui.js#mountWrapUpView renders perPlayerAccuracy AND interPlayerAgreement AND rankedDisagreements as three distinct sections.
Per-scenario optional note input (ui.js renderMyTurn, max 280 chars). Notes revealed in reveal panel AND wrap-up disagreement cards.
Web Push API + Notification API + service worker; no native build artifacts; click-gated permission request per iOS Safari requirement.
All 20 scenarios in data/scenarios.json are NLHE (board/action_history reference No-Limit Hold'em conventions: bb sizing, button position, c-bet sizing, etc.). No variant selector in UI.
scenarios.json is static curated data; no solver/CFR code anywhere; gto_action and gto_explanation are pre-written strings.
Anonymous Auth only (state.js:96 calls fbSignInAnonymously); display name is free-form input field. No email/password/OAuth call sites.
Per-game share code (6-char base32) embedded in join URL as ?join=<code>. createGame and joinGame both use the same gameId == shareCode.
All game state persisted in Firestore game document; readGame subscribes on cold-load; localStorage caches activeGameId only as a routing hint (app.js:31).
leaderUid alternation by i % 2 in joinGame's runTransaction (state.js:223). computePhase resolves current round and turn naturally from submission counts.
redactGameForViewer masks opponent submissions until viewer has submitted that round (state.js:413). Defense-in-depth against any client-side bug; Security Rules layer is independent.
README documents 4-step user setup: create Firebase project / paste config / push to GitHub / enable Pages.
grep for 'gto-poker-trainer' across output/integration: zero matches. Scenario data is embedded JSON, not a fetch from ancestor app.

out_of_scope_checks.evidence: No deposit/withdraw/wager/cashier surfaces in UI; no payment integration.
No variant selector; all 20 scenarios are NLHE.
Firestore Rules cap participantUids at size 2; joinGame rejects third UID with 'game_full'. Confirmed by edge report DCA.4.

out_of_scope_checks.oos: Synchronous real-time multiplayer with live timers
out_of_scope_checks.evidence: No countdown timer; no live-presence indicator; the entire model is turn-and-wait.
No .apk, .ipa, .xcodeproj files in integration directory. Web/PWA only.
Anonymous Auth only; zero non-anonymous auth call sites.
No CFR/equilibrium/solver code anywhere; all GTO answers are precomputed strings.
scenarios.json is read-only; no create/edit affordance in UI.
No import surface; no PokerStars/GGPoker integration.
No public sharing, no leaderboard, no community feed surfaces.
Reveal panels and wrap-up are bound to the two participants via Security Rules; no public link surface.
No report/ban/moderation UI; notes are just text fields between two known parties.
No server config, no DNS config, no CDN config files. User does GitHub Pages enablement themselves.
Zero references to gto-poker-trainer; scenarios are independent embedded JSON.

inflection_point_checks.chosen_branch: Firebase (Firestore + W3C Web Push via VAPID) Spark plan
inflection_point_checks.evidence: state.js imports firebase-app/auth/firestore from gstatic CDN; firestore.rules ships participants-only predicate; push.js implements client-side VAPID-signed Web Push (not FCM HTTP v1 API) per researcher recommendation.

inflection_point_checks.chosen_branch: Web Push API + service worker + W3C VAPID-signed push from submitting client
inflection_point_checks.evidence: sw.js registered at relative './sw.js' with scope './'; manifest.json display:'standalone'; permission request gated on user click; subscription endpoint POST'd with VAPID JWT signed via SubtleCrypto ECDSA P-256.
state.js#signInAnonymously called from app.js boot before any UI mount; no non-anonymous auth paths exist.

inflection_point_checks.chosen_branch: Per-game share code in join URL
inflection_point_checks.evidence: 6-char base32 share code, join URL = origin+pathname+?join=code, prefill in router on ?join detection.

inflection_point_checks.chosen_branch: Ported ancestor scenario library as static JSON
inflection_point_checks.evidence: 20 scenarios in data/scenarios.json, all required fields present (edge report S1.A1).
Inputs in create-game form, 1-10 range, persisted to game.config.handful_size.
textarea maxlength=280 in ui.js, note field in PlayerSubmission schema, rendered in reveal panel and disagreement cards.

inflection_point_checks.chosen_branch: Discrete 1-5 confidence scale with joint-min ranking
inflection_point_checks.evidence: Five conf buttons in renderMyTurn, rankedDisagreements uses Math.min(c_a, c_b) descending, edge report IP8.A2 confirms (5,5) > (5,2) ordering.

artifact_exercise_results.evidence: Each clause of the restatement maps to a verified code path — see PNV.1 static_trace_clauses.
Subpath-safe asset graph + relative sw.js registration + manifest with scope:'./' all confirm GitHub Pages hostability.
Edge report DCA.5: after A submits, A.waitingForOpponent=true and B.myTurn=true; B's view contains the same scenarioIds A saw.
precomputeRounds builds round.scenarioIds once at game-creation; both UIDs read from the same array.
handful_size=3 in DCA.5 edge report scenario: scenarioIds.length === 4 (4 was the chosen test value), persisted to game.config and respected by readGame redaction.
Edge report DCA.8: after both submit final round, computePhase returns gameComplete=true.
ui.js validation gate; state.js submitHandful schema check rejects missing confidence.
Edge report DCA.10 + S4.A7: (5,5) > (5,2) ordering confirmed.
Edge report DCA.11: interPlayerAgreement same=1, total=4, pct=25.
Note input and reveal rendering paths confirmed.
No sign-up UI anywhere; anonymous auth resolves silently at boot.
Both profiles operate on the same gameId/shareCode; participantUids array contains both UIDs.
All state in Firestore; cold-reload via app.js initial route resolution restores the active game from localStorage hint + Firestore read.
leaderUid alternation by round index; round leader submits first naturally because the responder cannot see scenarios until the leader has submitted (computePhase + redactGameForViewer).
Subpath-safe; no 404s on relative-path asset graph.
Landing screen with 'Create a new game' button rendered immediately after boot.
Share URL with ?join=<code> joins as second player.
Action and confidence required; note optional.
@media (max-width:480px) responsive overrides; viewport meta present.
Wrap-up renders all three sections (accuracy, agreement, disagreements).

edge_case_testing_pass_through.note: Edge case testing builder ran a Node ESM harness against the integration directory; results pass-through here as the closest available behavioral evidence under the verifier's no-browser constraint.

concerns.concern: Tier 1 PNV.1 could not be executed end-to-end live (no spawnable browser + no live Firebase project in verifier context). Static-trace + edge-case-testing report (32/32 PASS) substitute under the v1.9 honest-reporting contract. The first-contact path is genuinely exercised live by the user when they deploy.
concerns.mitigation: All structural and pure-logic clauses of PNV.1 verified offline. Live behavioral verification deferred to user's first deploy; FC.6 (push delivery) and FC.7 (real mobile rendering) are the items that genuinely cannot be statically verified.

concerns.concern: User must obtain a VAPID private key via a third-party tool (e.g., `npx web-push generate-vapid-keys`) per src/config.js comments. Firebase Console only surfaces the public half. Editor flagged this in concerns_for_editor; README and config.js comments document the workaround.
concerns.mitigation: Documented in README + config.js header comments. One-time setup, no architectural fix needed.

concerns.concern: EU iOS users may experience reduced Web Push support due to Apple DMA changes; not a build defect, a platform constraint. Onboarding copy flags the iOS Add-to-Home-Screen requirement universally.
concerns.mitigation: Per Editor concern; not a blocker for the prompt's two-known-users scenario.

concerns.concern: Client-side VAPID signing in push.js uses SubtleCrypto + manual JWK construction from raw 32-byte d + uncompressed P-256 public key. This is correct per RFC 8292 but cryptographically subtle — if either key half is malformed the signing throws at runtime and sendTurnNotification gracefully returns reason:'send_failed'. Code includes try/catch wrappers so app remains functional even on misconfigured VAPID keys.
concerns.mitigation: Graceful degradation already implemented (push failures don't block gameplay).

summary.tier1_pnv: structural pass + edge-case-testing report (32/32) cited as closest behavioral evidence; live end-to-end execution deferred per v1.9 honest-reporting contract
summary.tier3_subgoal: all sampled DCA / IP / acceptance assertions pass; 32/32 edge case results pass-through
summary.principle_h_skips: none — no td_plan assertion was used to make an external-system property claim outside what was already independently verified via canonical evidence
summary.concerns: 4 minor (production-fidelity gap inherent to verifier env; one-time VAPID setup burden; EU iOS DMA platform constraint; cryptographic subtlety in client-side VAPID signing — all gracefully handled)
summary.recommendation: PASS WITH CONCERNS. Artifact is deliverable. The two known live-only verifications (FC.6 push delivery on a real Chrome/Edge instance and FC.7 mobile rendering on a real touchscreen) are inherent first-contact items the user will exercise on first deploy; they are static-trace-correct here.
